result.summary: Firestore exposes a public MCP server endpoint at `https://firestore.googleapis.com/mcp` plus the Cloud Firestore REST API at `https://firestore.googleapis.com/v1`, which accepts either Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens or Firebase Authentication ID tokens.
result.description: Cloud Firestore is Google's managed NoSQL document database for Firebase and Google Cloud applications. It stores and synchronizes structured document data and supports querying, transactions, and realtime application backends.

google_oauth_access_token.label: Google Identity OAuth 2.0 access token
google_oauth_access_token.setup: Create or use a Google Cloud project in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), enable Firestore access for that project, and obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token using Google's standard application or user credential flows. For server-to-server use, the easiest path is usually to create a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and use Google Application Default Credentials or service-account-based OAuth to mint a bearer token. Firestore docs state REST requests accept a Google Identity OAuth 2.0 token in the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

firebase_id_token.label: Firebase Authentication ID token
firebase_id_token.setup: Create a Firebase project in the [Firebase console](https://console.firebase.google.com/), enable [Firebase Authentication](https://firebase.google.com/docs/auth), and sign a user in through Firebase Auth to obtain that user's ID token. Firestore docs state REST requests can use a Firebase Authentication ID token as a bearer token in the `Authorization: Bearer` header; the request is then evaluated under that signed-in user's permissions.
